text: I keep hearing the claim that Dubai is dead and that all the wealth has fled the city. It couldn’t be the farthest thing from the truth. https://t.co/BWQlOIXHLO
html:
Data exists. Dubai attracted a record 9,800 millionaires in 2025 ($63B wealth inflow, per Henley & Partners)—more than any country. Tourism hit 19.59M visitors (record high, +5% YoY). Economy forecast +4.5% GDP growth in 2026 (Emirates NBD/DET), with $29B budget signaling investment.

Regional tensions (Iran conflict) have softened some segments like luxury real estate (-8-12% in spots) and tourism, but outflows are limited—not a "dead city" exodus. Video's luxury cars reflect ongoing high-end activity, not hype.

text:
If you run a remote agency doing $30k+ per month, you are probably losing $120k to $200k a year to income tax.

That money could be hiring two senior team members. Or your entire annual profit margin.

Here is why agency owners are quietly relocating to the UAE.
html:
Banking is where most agency owners stumble.

You need multi-currency SWIFT receiving (USD, EUR, GBP) from global clients.

Mashreq, Emirates NBD, and Wio offer this. But approval rates vary wildly.

Getting bank selection right the first time saves months.

text: If you’re running production in AWS ME-CENTRAL-1 or ME-SOUTH-1, this week should’ve triggered a hard review of your DR plan. Reports say mec1-az2 and mec1-az3 in the UAE were impaired, and Bahrain’s ME-SOUTH-1 took another hit on April 1. Single-region Gulf deployments need a second look. #AWS #SysAdmin
html: Multi-AZ inside one region won’t save you if the region itself is taking physical damage. UAE banking and payments already showed the blast radius, with reported outages hitting ADCB, Emirates NBD, Alaan, Hubpay, and Careem during the AWS disruption.

post:
I was shocked today by something unacceptable from Emirates Islamic Bank.

I was previously contacted and offered a 6-month deferment of installments, and based on this promise, I arranged my financial commitments and upcoming plans. However, when I logged into the app, I was stunned to find that the actual deferment applied is only 3 months!

This behavior raises many questions:
Are the offers made to customers just inaccurate promises?
Where is the transparency in dealings?
And how can a financial decision be based on incorrect information?

What happened is not just a simple mistake; it is a breach of trust and falls within the scope of misleading offers.

I demand an immediate clarification from Emirates Islamic Bank and a correction of this situation, adhering to what was agreed upon without any changes or manipulation.
